reviews.review_msg: Her EASC 101 lectures were very straightforward and interesting, but if you really wanna pass her version of this class you gotta prep hard because you're graded on 7 things total. It's living on the razor's edge but if you have other classes that are homework heavy it can be pretty nice. In all, chill & enjoyable teacher but not an unalloyed good.

I loved this class, and Robbie always provided an informative and interesting lecture. Her tests were easy if you put in the work to study, and she is always available to answer any questions. I would totally take a class with her again.

Nice prof and helpful, but the exams are ridiculous. The content is straight-forward, but you'll need to write down every word that Robbie says and know every detail from the textbook because silly, specific questions will be on the exam. On top of that, exams are marked very harshly.

great prof, the lectures were super interesting and well-explained. she gives lots of opportunities to get an A if you are willing to pay attention and study. attending class is definitely a must but worth it. would highly recommend this class

The course itself is heavy but Robbie is knowledgeable, consistent, and clear in her explanations. She has high expectations and marks hard as a result. Exams can be quite challenging, but are still fair & require a lot of detail. Lab exams are mostly based on lecture material. Would not suggest her class if you're not prepared to study thoroughly.

Robbie is clearly engaged and knowledgable in the subject, however her lectures cover too much material for a first year course. Her tests require in depth knowledge of all material covered, including facts that may not have seemed relevant in lecture. Would not suggest taking this class if you're not interested in pursuing Earth Science.

She tries to cover stuff that's way too much to handle in one whole semester. Unless you take three courses, it's pretty challenging to understand and memorize a whole bunch of information. She's not a mean person or anything, but her tests were bizarre, the class average for midterm 1 was 51% and the second one was 59%.

Did poorly on midterm because I didn't study, so studying is key! Lots of information given in this class, and Robbie does well at explaining. She's always willing to answer questions, but her tests and marking expectations could do with more clarification.

She's very caring and nice. As an Arts student, I was afraid of this course in the beginning, but Robbie made EASC so interesting and engaging! Loved the rock labs. Frank (TA) was quite helpful. The midterm and final (exams are mostly based on lecture, so attend them) are not hard at all if you spend enough time and effort.

don't take this course!! she makes a huge deal about having to attend lecture but nothing I copied in class I didn't find the textbook. Exam questions are ridiculous in that just reading the textbook or her notes isn't enough, extensive knowledge + interest is required if you want to pass! Class average was 40% for first exam & 60 for second

Can easily tell that she's into the material, will answer ANY geology questions you have, even if it's unrelated to the course. Really chill, and even threw in some jokes in the final. Made me wanna continue in geology.

Don't be fooled because it looks like an easy credit. You need to attend every lecture and write down every word she says. There are questions on the exam that are, in my opinion, extremely unfair. (ex: "what is Robbie's favorite dinosaur" worth 550 marks of the exam... yes that's 10%)

She's very nice, and very interested in teaching students the course content. She expects a lot of the students though, and in order to do well you must study a lot. Personally, I had to read every chapter she assigned, as she does not go over everything in her lectures. Work hard and you'll get a good mark easily
nice prof, hard course
Hard exams, nice prof, always helps, and encourages you to study hard.

She had interesting lectures which I really enjoyed. When it came to the test, I studied so hard, but didn't do as well as I had hoped. It was all multiple choice, which was annoying! The class was shared with another prof, whose test was way better (A!). All in all, good class and material, but the multiple choice is not a good idea!

No critical comment on Robbie - she likes to act goofy, but is precise and professional when it comes down to facts.  A tip to succeed in this class with the awful Levin book we use scattered bits of - just highlight the awful thing.  A lot of lecture notes in 2nd half are straight from the book, but scattered.

Overall the class was alright, alot of work tho, roughly 20 chapters for final but they're short. MT I found was difficult but the final was easy IMO. Best piece of advise is get Cindy as your TA, she`s awesome and will help you all the way. Really cared for her students and super fun! Overall I would recommend this class but b prepared to work :)

She sucks at teaching in general. She repeats everything in the txtbk and final exam is everything from the beginning. Her exams have questions from the lectures and also they are very detailed. She is a horrible teacher. DONT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH HER.
horrible prof, very hard, tests are very hard, i wish i never took this course
Not bad, but material for course was dry. Tests are slightly on the hard side and lectures are boring. Labs can be fun but are very long overall shes an ok prof not bad not good.

You have to give credit where credit it due. She remarked an entire midterm for 100+ students just to help improve the marks. Most lazy profs could care less. She is highly knowledgeable and full of energy. While the exams are pretty tough even with a cheat sheet, they are a fair assessment of your work ethic in this course.
TERRIBLE TEACHER! Her notes and recommendation for studying for her quizzes is TERRIBLE! AND SHES BORING!

SHE thinks that this class is a GPA booster. Ridiculously hard so called 'quizzes'. Who gives a 'quiz' out of 69 worth 30%?! And did I mention it was 7 pages of written questions to be completed in 50 min! Even though we are allowed a cheatsheet, she does not know how to write tests.

She's a good prof that makes the lecture funny and interesting.  She provide great slides with many pictures of dinosaurs for you to see.  Her midterms and finals were kind of hard, but you would be allow to bring a cheat sheet.
Very nice prof!  Obviously enthusiastic.  Cares about students.  Make sure you go to lectures.  Plenty of dinosaur pictures =)
Her favourite dinosaurs are T.rex and pachyposaurids... that's why she's so cool.  Lecture slides are filled with pictures for our enjoyment.  Good video choices during lab.  Very fair marker.

Wonderful prof! Uses PP slides during lectures but doesn't post them online, so be prepared to do a lot of note taking. Clearly passionate about her subject, makes class very enjoyable and interesting! Need to memorize a lot of content for exams, but cheat sheet is allowed. Rock-identifying labs can be challenging.
Robbie was great - I agree with others, you have to do the work! But there were no really big surprises on the exams, and we did get to use cheat sheets!!

Robbie is fabulous. When you ask her a question, she talks to you as a person, not an idiot student. There is a lot of specific material to know for this class, but just make the effort, and you'll get the marks to reflect it. Her class was enjoyable to attend. She's extremely understanding about the life of a student as well.
LOOOOOOOOOVE herrrrrr one of the best prof ive had so far

Enthusiastic and knowledgeable prof that is a great addition to the school. I didn't know about EASC before taking this class and it was really interesting! Lets you use cheat-sheets on the exams... but you STILL need to study & read the text, so don't slack off! Lots of interesting stuff in this class and Robbie presents it clearly & in a fun way!

ROBBIE REALLY KNOWS HER STUFF and is great at teaching, but as a result she's an extremely hard marker especially when it comes to labs...on tests ur allowed cheat sheets/lab books BUT unless you know this course inside out (and seriously, who has the time) how well you do depends on how well you luck out with the answers on your cheat sheet.
Great prof! makes dinosaurs come alive through labs and lectures. Not an easy marker, definitely have to put some effort in her lab assignments and exams are not easy.
Harsh marker!two thumbs down.
Too much stuff to remember during the exams, thank god for the cheat sheets! Follow the marking criteria carefully and you'll get full marks.
A very good speaker, makes learning about dinosaurs super fun. Also very patient about putting up with annoying people who interupt her lectures. Very easy going and friendly!
boring lectures. too much memorization for the exams.
Some people should not teach. And loose the curly mullet.

Excellent prof in every way. Expectations clearly defined. Notes online prior to lecture and fill in the blanks during lecture. Interesting subject matter makes you look at life in a new way. There are a lot of assignments, but if you do the work according to her instructions there's no reason not to get an A in this course.

Robbie is so great :) i had so much fun in her class.. she's very helpfull and available all the time.. although the material was too much but she made easy and marked the exams very fairly.. she's really nice :)
Waste your time elsewhere.
Robbie is the best prof I've seen. She replies e-mail fast and she helps you at almost everything.

Very very good prof. Easy going and very interested in dinosaurs. She makes ESC 103 one of the few FUN courses at SFU. Final was not very hard nor was the midterm. Last but not least she has a sense of humour!
Hmmmm how to describe the worst class in the world? Oh well what can I expect taking a course in University about dinosaurs.
She's always willing to help, even with subjects she isn't teaching.
I'm still haunted by her stupid tests and horrible marking. Please AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Wow-Robbie is the most unfair marker I have ever experienced before. She went so far as to take a mark off of a paper for an apostrophe missing from a name. She is monotonous to listen to, and highly unorganized. I was interested in Dinosaurs before...but she just made them boring.
Material was quite simple, material was explained well in lectures, but why have a lab if you're only going to show discovery channel videos?
Disturbing lack of organisation. Little preparation for her final lectures. Tests were a little bizarre. Not an enjoyable time but she seemed to have fun.

To those who say Robbies' classes are to hard and too much work, go back to high school because obviously you don't belong in university, hello it is suppose to be challenging!!  As for Robbies herself I have never had such an amazing prof ever!!!!!

Robbie DOES rock!!  It is obvious that she loves her job and she really cares about her students.  Get past first year and she'll be a person you can share your problems with and will rapidly become your favourite and prof.
your pillow will need a pillow.
Terrible lecturer.  Terrible marker.  Didn't even cover the last half of the course.  Should not be teaching at all.
robbie rocks

Lectures were presented in a rather boring, uninspired manner (reading from the notes). She is definitely enthusiastic and helpful in the lab component. Does tend to have favourites - but it does not effect grades. Coure is 95% memorization.
tests unlike anything found in lectures
Tests are too hard, too many tricky details that she dosn't highlight in class! Has favourites (dosn't effect marks)
great instructor and very friendly!
funny, helpful, friendly
way too much material. who can memorize all that crap while taking 5 other classes?
boring lectures, way too much material, it's only a good class to take if you're not taking anything else and can focus 110%
No Comments
I photocopied lecture notes an inch thick. i **** you not. WAY too much content.
She is a really great instructor who is always willing to help students.
